title: CXXRTL: Fix sliced bits as clock inputs
body:
Currently in cxxrtl due to an earlier change that fixed issues with constant signals driving clock inputs of clocked cells, when cells are clocked by a `SigSpec` that doesn't pass `is_wire()` most of them will be ignored. 

This causes an issue because slices of another signal (i.e. a single `SigBit`) don't return `true` for `is_wire()`. While it's arguable that there should be some `is_wire()` like function defined that passes for slices (at least for single bits), that's another issue.

In order to fix #2542, I implemented a subset of `is_wire()` internally to the cxxrtl backend, so that it can handle cells with clocks that are not directly backed by a wire. This doesn't fully convert all uses of `is_wire()` for clocks. Some of the code can map them onto their unique driving signal at the point of testing, which I think should be equivalent, but someone might want to verify that.
